title: ipomoea orizabensis subsp. austromexicana
intro: Ipomoea orizabensis subsp. austromexicana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the morning glory family, known for its vibrant purple flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its high content of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ipomoea orizabensis subsp. austromexicana include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
